title: Variables & Data Types
objectives: Declare variables with let and const and understand when to use each
Work with strings, numbers, booleans, arrays, and objects
Use template literals to build dynamic strings
hints: Use const for values that never change (like a tax rate), let for values that do (like a running total)
Template literals use backticks and ${} — `Hello, ${name}!` inserts the variable's value
Arrays hold ordered lists: ['apple', 'banana']. Objects hold named properties: { name: 'Ada', age: 30 }
assessmentCriteria: Variables are declared with const and let appropriately (no var)
At least 3 different data types are used
Template literals are used for string building
An array and an object are created with real-world data
